questionHtml:
A shopkeeper marked an item 80% above its cost price. He then offered two successive discounts of 20% and 25%. If he made a profit of Rs. 160, at what price did he sell the item ?

  1. Rs. 2000
  2. Rs. 2500
  3. Rs. 2160
  4. Rs. 1000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A mobile shop owner buys 10 boxes of mobiles for the price of 8 boxes from a wholesaler. If he sells each box at a 2% discount on its marked price, what is his profit percentage?

  1. 10%
  2. 24.36%
  3. 22.5%
  4. 23.86%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A fruit seller offers mangoes to a retailer at a 30% discount on the marked price. However, they charge an additional 5% on the discounted price for shipping. The retailer sells the mangoes for Rs. 1620 more, earning a profit of 40%. What was the marked price of the mangoes?

  1. Rs. 5,510.20
  2. Rs. 9,254.62
  3. Rs. 8,181.82
  4. Rs. 20,654.67
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
P, Q, and R invested 12,000, Rs. 16,000, and Rs. 20,000 respectively. P's investment was for 12 months, Q's for 8 months, and R's for 4 months. The total profit is Rs. 55,000. What is Q's share of the profit ?

  1. Rs. 20,000
  2. Rs. 30,000
  3. Rs. 25,000
  4. Rs. 32,000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A Smart watch retailer sells a Smart watch for Rs. P and earns a profit of 20%. For a special festive offer, he marks the same Smart watch at Rs. 1.5P. At the offer, he provides a discount of 10%. What will be the percentage profit that he will make during the festive offer?

  1. 36%
  2. 46%
  3. 62%
  4. 60%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A gadget is sold at a 25% discount on its marked price. This sale leads to a profit of 10% on its cost price. If the profit earned is Rs. 300, what would have been the selling price if the gadget was sold at a 20% discount on its marked price?

  1. Rs. 3520
  2. Rs. 3720
  3. Rs. 2,879.45
  4. Rs. 3320
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
An item costs Rs. 300. A customer gets a 20% discount on it. If the seller still makes a 25% profit, what is the cost price of the item?

  1. Rs. 192
  2. Rs. 209
  3. Rs. 186
  4. Rs. 159
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A web developer sells a premium website template to a distributor at a 25% discount. An additional 10% service fee is added to the discounted price. The distributor then sells the template for Rs. 9000 more, earning a 20% profit. What is the original marked price of the template fixed by the developer?

  1. Rs. 55,550.45
  2. Rs. 57,545.35
  3. Rs. 56,540.25
  4. Rs. 54,545.45
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
The marked price of a TV is Rs. 30,000. Amit receives two successive discounts while buying it. If the first discount is 20% and the final price he pays is Rs. 16,800, what is the second discount rate?

  1. 30%
  2. 16%
  3. 14%
  4. 15%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
एक वेब डेवलपर एक प्रीमियम वेबसाइट टेम्पलेट को एक वितरक (distributor) को 25% की छूट पर बेचता है।छूट वाले मूल्य पर 10% की अतिरिक्त सेवा शुल्क (service fee) जोड़ी जाती है। वितरक उस टेम्पलेट को Rs. 9000 अधिक में बेचता है और 20% का लाभ प्राप्त करता है। तो डेवलपर द्वारा तय किया गया टेम्पलेट का मूल अंकित मूल्य (marked price) ज्ञात कीजिए।

  1. Rs. 55,550.45
  2. Rs. 57,545.35
  3. Rs. 56,540.25
  4. Rs. 54,545.45
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A manufacturer marked a product 20% above its cost price. He provided a discount to a customer, which resulted in him making a profit of 8%. What was the rate of discount given by the manufacturer?

  1. 22%
  2. 40%
  3. 30%
  4. 10%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A and B invest Rs. 60,000 and Rs. 80,000 respectively. A remains in business for 8 months, B for 9 months. If the total profit is Rs. 40,000, what is A's share ?

  1. Rs. 16,000
  2. Rs. 18,000
  3. Rs. 20,000
  4. Rs. 24,000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A vendor purchases 5 shirts at the marked price of 4 shirts. If he sells each shirt after giving a 8% discount on its marked price, what is his profit percentage ?

  1. 10%
  2. 11%
  3. 15%
  4. 13%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
The cost price of an article is Rs. 200. A shopkeeper sells it at a discount of 20% on the marked price. If the marked price is Rs. 400, what is the shopkeeper's profit or loss percentage?

  1. 60% Profit
  2. 60% Loss
  3. 30% Profit
  4. 30% Loss
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A fruit vendor sells 20 kg of oranges for Rs. 900, thereby gaining the cost price of 5 kg of oranges. What is his profit percentage ?

  1. 28.65%
  2. 25%
  3. 26%
  4. 31.56%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A refrigerator has a marked price of Rs. 15,000. It is sold with two successive discounts. If the second discount is 30% and the final selling price is Rs. 9,000, what is the percentage of the first discount ?

  1. 12.5%
  2. 12%
  3. 14.28%
  4. 15%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A furniture store marks its items at 60% above the cost price. They offer a discount of 20% on the marked price. If a customer receives an additional loyalty discount of Rs. 221, and the store still makes a 15% profit on the cost price, what is the cost price of the furniture item ?

  1. Rs. 1,700
  2. Rs. 2,500
  3. Rs. 1,500
  4. Rs. 2,200
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A bookseller purchases 6 novels at the marked price of 8 novels from a distributor. If he then sells these novels to customers, offering a discount of 15% on the marked price, what is his profit percentage ?

  1. 11%
  2. 21.75%
  3. 19.62%
  4. 13.34%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A shopkeeper marked a mobile phone at Rs. 800. He sold it at a 15% discount. If his profit on the sale was 20%, what was the mobile phone's purchase price?

  1. Rs. 550.36
  2. Rs. 555.47
  3. Rs. 566.67
  4. Rs. 570.34
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A product is sold at a 20% discount on its marked price. This transaction results in a profit of 8% on its cost price. If the profit earned is Rs. 224, what would have been the selling price if the product was sold at a 33.33% discount on its marked price?

  1. Rs. 2587.50
  2. Rs. 3000.20
  3. Rs. 2520.00
  4. Rs. 2510.00
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A retailer marked a product 60% above its cost price. He provided two successive discounts of 25% and 10%. If he earned a profit of Rs. 240, what was the cost price of the product?

  1. Rs. 2000
  2. Rs. 1500
  3. Rs. 2700
  4. Rs. 3000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A trader marked an article 30% above its cost price. He then allowed a discount of 10% on the marked price. If the selling price of the article was Rs. 702, what was its cost price ?

  1. Rs. 600
  2. Rs. 880
  3. Rs. 950
  4. Rs. 550
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A book, originally priced at Rs. 800, is subject to a reduction in cost through two consecutive discounts: an initial markdown of 30%, followed by an additional 20% discount. The question then is to determine the conclusive selling price of the book once both these successive price adjustments have been applied.

  1. Rs. 432
  2. Rs. 396
  3. Rs. 448
  4. Rs. 350
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Having initially established the marked price of a particular commodity at a 25% premium over its cost price, a retail vendor subsequently extended an unspecified discount. If, following this concession, the transaction ultimately yielded a profit of 20% on the cost price, what was the exact percentage of the discount given ?

  1. 8%
  2. 4%
  3. 5%
  4. 10%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A software company developed 1600 licenses for a new application at a total cost of Rs. 8,000,000. They provided 100 licenses free to their top beta testers. For the remaining licenses, they offered a 30% discount on the market price of Rs. 12000 per license. Additionally, they gave 1 license free for every 9 licenses bought. If all 1600 licenses were distributed, what is the company's overall gain or loss percentage?

  1. 45.64% profit
  2. 41.75% profit
  3. 43.30% loss
  4. 43.64% loss
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A yojana has three stakeholders. P brought 60% of the capital, and Q brought the rest. R joined later and was promised 30% of the total profit for branding. If total profit was Rs. 3,50,000, how much was Q's share ?

  1. Rs. 84,000
  2. Rs. 90,000
  3. Rs. 98,000
  4. Rs. 1,05,000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A businessman invests Rs. 40,000 in buying a bike and spends Rs. 8,000 on transportation and Rs. 7,000 on storage. If he sells the bike for Rs. 60,000, what is the profit or loss percentage ?

  1. 9.09 % Profit
  2. 8.42% loss
  3. 8.26% loss
  4. 5.26% Profit
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A can contains 30 litres of oil at Rs. 50/litre. Another can contains 70 litres of oil at Rs. 50/litre. They are mixed, and 25 litres are sold at Rs. 60/litre. How much profit or loss does the seller make on this sale ?

  1. Rs. 600 profit
  2. Rs. 250 loss
  3. Rs. 280 loss
  4. Rs. 250 profit
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A company produced 6000 chocolates at a total cost of Rs. 180,000. They distributed 600 chocolates for free as samples. For the remaining chocolates, they offered a 10% discount on the market price of Rs. 40 per chocolate. Additionally, they gave 1 chocolate free with every 19 chocolates sold. If all 6000 chocolates were distributed, what is their overall profit or loss percentage ?

  1. 5.6% profit
  2. 2.6% profit
  3. 2.6% loss
  4. 5.6% loss
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A and B invested Rs. 80,000 and Rs. 60,000 respectively for 12 months. A take 10% of the profit for managing. The remaining profit of Rs. 1,40,000 is divided based on capital. What is B's share ?

  1. Rs. 6,0000
  2. Rs. 30,000
  3. Rs. 40,000
  4. Rs. 35,000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A person invested in two items - one gave a 8% profit and the other a 6% loss. If the total investment was Rs. 6,000 and the net result was a Rs. 200 profit, what was the investment in the profit-making item?

  1. Rs. 4,000
  2. Rs. 3,500
  3. Rs. 4,500
  4. Rs. 3,000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A wholesaler sells a packet of tea to a retailer at a profit of 25%. The retailer then marks up the price by 40% and offers a discount of 10% to the customer. If the customer pays Rs. 630 , find the cost price of the packet for the wholesaler.

  1. Rs. 600
  2. Rs. 400
  3. Rs. 560
  4. Rs. 650
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
P and Q started a business by investing Rs. 60,000 and Rs. 30,000 respectively. After 3 months, R joined with Rs. 45,000. At the end of the year, what is R's share in a profit of Rs. 40,000 (approx.)?

  1. Rs. 10,909
  2. Rs. 10,109
  3. Rs. 15,909
  4. Rs. 15,109
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
D and E start a business with equal investments. After 6 months, D adds Rs. 15,000 more. At the end of 1 year, if the total profit is Rs. 35,000, and E's share is Rs. 10,000, what was their initial investment?

  1. Rs. 6000
  2. Rs. 4000
  3. Rs. 5000
  4. Rs. 7000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A person bought a cycle at a discount of 25% on the marked price. He spent Rs. 510 on repairs and sold it for Rs. 5760, gaining 20%. What was the marked price?

  1. Rs. 5,500
  2. Rs. 6,000
  3. Rs. 5,600
  4. Rs. 5,720
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Amit buys a 3 identical watches at a total cost of Rs. 3,500. He sells them at Rs. 1,600, Rs. 1,700, and Rs. 1,500, respectively. What is the overall profit or loss?

  1. Rs. 1100 loss
  2. Rs. 1300 profit
  3. Rs. 1600 profit
  4. Rs. 1600 loss
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
An electronics dealer marks an item 50% above cost and offers a festival discount of 20%, plus a cashback of Rs. 200 on the final selling price. If he still makes a 15% profit, what is the cost price ?

  1. Rs. 4300
  2. Rs. 4000
  3. Rs. 4100
  4. Rs. 4200
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A seller offers a combo deal: "Buy 4 shirts for the price of 3". If each shirt costs him Rs. 1500, what is the effective loss percentage ?

  1. 20%

  3. 30%
  4. 33 % 1 3
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
If the selling price of an item is increased by 10 % and the profit also increases from 15 % to 20%, what is the percentage increase in the cost price ?

  1. 3.5%
  2. 4.6%
  3. 1.28%
  4. 5.41%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A seller offers a 30% discount on an item whose cost price is Rs. 800. Despite the discount, the seller earns a profit of 25%. What must be the marked price?

  1. Rs. 1,420
  2. Rs. 1,430
  3. Rs. 1,440
  4. Rs. 1,450
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
P and Q share profit in 9 : 7. If P gets Rs. 27,000, how much did Q receive?

  1. Rs. 21,500
  2. Rs. 10,000
  3. Rs. 21,000
  4. Rs. 22,000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A man made a 20% profit on his investment. If he invested Rs. 10,000 and reinvested the profit, what would his net worth be after the second year at the same rate of return ?

  1. Rs. 16000
  2. Rs. 16050
  3. Rs. 14,400
  4. Rs. 14150
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A shopkeeper bought 200 toys at Rs 50 each. He sold 80% of the toys at 10% profit and the rest at 10% loss. What is the overall profit or loss percentage?

  1. 8% profit
  2. 6% profit
  3. 5% loss
  4. 6% loss
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A vendor offers a 10% discount on the marked price and sells a packet of salty biscuits for Rs. 180. If the profit earned on this sale is 50%, find the cost price.

  1. Rs 136
  2. Rs 120
  3. Rs 130
  4. Rs 110
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A trader gave a 25 % discount on the market price but still earned a 15 % profit. What percentage was the market price above the cost price ?

  1. 45.5%
  2. 60%
  3. 43.34%
  4. 53.34%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A distributor marks a batch of 220 identical mobile chargers at 45% above the cost price and offers a 15% discount to retailers. A retailer purchases all the chargers and spends Rs. 14,790. What is the cost price per charger for the wholesaler ?

  1. Rs. 54.5
  2. Rs. 57.5
  3. Rs. 56.5
  4. Rs. 55.5
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
एक वितरक 220 समान मोबाइल चार्जर के एक बैच पर लागत मूल्य से 45% अधिक मूल्य अंकित करता है और खुदरा विक्रेताओं को 15% की छूट देता है।एक खुदरा विक्रेता सभी चार्जर खरीदता है और इसके लिए Rs. 14,790 खर्च करता है। थोक विक्रेता के लिए प्रति चार्जर क्रय मूल्य (Cost Price) क्या है?

  1. Rs. 54.5
  2. Rs. 57.5
  3. Rs. 56.5
  4. Rs. 55.5
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
An investment is made in a business. In the first year, it gains 30%, in the second year, it loses 25%, and in the third year, it gains 20%. What is the overall gain or loss percentage ?

  1. 20% gain
  2. 20% loss
  3. 22% gain
  4. 17% gain
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A shopkeeper sells two shirts at Rs. 600 each. On one, he gains 20%, and on the other, he loses 20%. What is his net gain or loss?

  1. Rs. 50 loss
  2. Rs. 75 gain
  3. Rs. 40 gain
  4. Rs. 80 loss
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Due to an error, a shopkeeper increases the selling price of a Rs 400 item by 12.5 % instead of decreasing it by 12.5 %. What is the percentage increase in the price due to this error ?

  1. 18.22%
  2. 24.22%
  3. 25.22%
  4. 28.57%
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
If the price tag of a microwave is Rs. 4500 and the shopkeeper offers a discount of 19%, what is the selling price?

  1. Rs. 3545
  2. Rs. 3645
  3. Rs. 4345
  4. Rs. 4200
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A man buys 10 shirts for Rs. 5,000 and sells 8 of them for Rs. 4,900. If he discards the remaining 2 shirts, what is his overall gain or loss ?

  1. Rs. 100 loss
  2. Rs. 50 loss
  3. Rs. 100 profit
  4. Rs. 50 profit
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
If the selling price of an article is 85% of its marked price and the cost price is 75% of the marked price, what is the profit percentage?

  1. 14 %

  3. 16 %
  4. 13 % 1 7 1 3 2 3 2 3

questionHtml:

questionHtml:

  3. Rs. 2,180

questionHtml:

  4. Rs. 4,173.07

  3. Rs. 240
  4. Rs. 260

questionHtml:

  4. Rs. 32,500

questionHtml:

  1. Rs. 600
  2. Rs. 400
  3. Rs. 640
  4. Rs. 750
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A distributor marks a batch of 280 identical mobile chargers at 60% above the cost price and offers a 20% discount to retailers. A retailer purchases all the chargers and spends Rs. 15,360. What is the cost price per charger for the wholesaler ?

  1. Rs. 42.85
  2. Rs. 57.75
  3. Rs. 56.65
  4. Rs. 55.55
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
एक वितरक 280 समान मोबाइल चार्जर के एक बैच पर लागत मूल्य से 60% अधिक मूल्य अंकित करता है और खुदरा विक्रेताओं को 20% की छूट देता है। एक खुदरा विक्रेता सभी चार्जर खरीदता है और इसके लिए Rs. 15,360 खर्च करता है। थोक विक्रेता के लिए प्रति चार्जर क्रय मूल्य (Cost Price) क्या है ?

  1. Rs. 42.85

  4. Rs. 55.55

questionHtml:
A furniture establishment initially disposes of a particular dining chair for Rs. X, thereby realising a 25% profit margin. Subsequently, as a promotional strategy for a festive period, the marked price of this identical chair is elevated to Rs. 1.8X. Following this adjustment, a special concession of 20% is applied to the newly established marked price. The inquiry then seeks to ascertain the resulting percentage profit generated by the store during this specific celebratory sales period. What is the percentage profit earned by the store during the festive sale ?

  1. 40%
  2. 80%
  3. 42%
  4. 70%
subject: Mathematics
